phase_note: Phase 1 focuses on literal parsing, escape handling, and early unterminated-token diagnostics before widening to full literal normalization.

cases.upstream_focus: Malformed source snippets should fail during parsing with location-aware diagnostics instead of surviving into later execution stages.
cases.zr_decision: ZR rejects unterminated or structurally broken literal forms during parse and treats them as non-recoverable for the current compilation unit.

cases.upstream_focus: EOF inside single-quoted and double-quoted strings must raise a dedicated unterminated-literal syntax error.
cases.zr_decision: ZR should reject an unclosed string token immediately when the lexer/parser reaches end-of-file.

cases.upstream_focus: Escaped end-quote and unterminated literal diagnostics should stay precise instead of collapsing into a generic parse failure.
cases.zr_decision: ZR keeps a dedicated fixture for unclosed strings so later compiler passes never see a partial string AST node.

cases.upstream_focus: Valid literal escape combinations need a positive baseline alongside negative EOF diagnostics.
cases.zr_decision: ZR literal coverage should pair failing fixtures with future escape-sequence success fixtures in the same matrix.

cases.upstream_focus: The parser must stop on unterminated double-quoted strings even in the presence of indentation or tab alignment noise.
cases.zr_decision: ZR treats unterminated string literals as parser-level failures independent of surrounding whitespace layout.

cases.upstream_focus: General language baselines should keep literal evaluation and parse behavior exercised under executable tests, not documentation only.
cases.zr_decision: ZR keeps literal inventory in manifests first, then grows runtime literal fixtures from the same matrix.
